kitchen.pnEncryptionWarning: Secure key storage is not configured. Contact your system administrator to enable PrintNode integration.
kitchen.pnGuideTitle: First time setting up? Read this first
kitchen.pnGuideSubtitle: Create a PrintNode account, install the desktop app, get your API key — 5 minute walkthrough
kitchen.pnHowItWorksBody: There are two pieces. They can live on the same device or on two devices that share a network — your call.
kitchen.pnPieceKitchenApp: Kitchen Order App — a free Android app on Google Play (search “Fee Free Order App”), with WiFi/LAN printing built in. It also runs in any browser on any device: iPad, Android tablet, iPhone, Windows tablet, laptop, desktop, even a TV with a Chromecast — just open the site and log in, and use Add to Home Screen on a tablet.
kitchen.pnPiecePrintNode: PrintNode — the bridge that talks to your physical receipt printer. Only runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, or Raspberry Pi. No iOS or Android version exists. Must be on a device on the same network as the printer (USB connection is fine — usually the same device).
kitchen.pnSetupCheapest: Cheapest / simplest: One Windows mini-PC (~$150) runs both — kitchen display in a browser and PrintNode + USB printer.
kitchen.pnSetupTablet: Tablet kitchen: iPad or Android tablet for the display + a separate Raspberry Pi (~$60) or any spare PC for PrintNode + printer on the same Wi-Fi.
kitchen.pnSetupExisting: Already have a Mac/PC: Use it. PrintNode runs alongside whatever else is on it. The browser can be on the same machine or a separate tablet.
kitchen.pnStep1Body: Sign up with the email you want PrintNode notifications to go to. <warn>Each restaurant (and each location, if you have multiple) needs its own PrintNode account + paid plan.</warn> You can't share one account across locations because the API key only points to one set of installed printers. The free tier includes 50 prints / month for testing; production plans start around $5/mo for 500 prints.
kitchen.pnStep2Title: Open the Kitchen Order App on the device you want to view orders on
kitchen.pnStep2Body: No install needed — it's a web app. Open your browser, navigate to /kitchen on this site, sign in with the kitchen-staff account. On iPad / Android, use the browser's Add to Home Screen menu to pin the page — it then launches full-screen like a native app. On Windows you can do the same via Chrome / Edge → Install App.
kitchen.pnStep3Title: Install PrintNode on the device connected to your printer
kitchen.pnStep3Body: This device must be Windows, macOS, Linux, or Raspberry Pi (PrintNode doesn't ship for iOS or Android). It must stay powered on with PrintNode running — it's the bridge between us and your printer. Once installed, sign in with the account from step 1. PrintNode auto-detects any printer your operating system already recognizes — USB Star TSP143, Epson TM, network thermal, regular office printers, all work.
kitchen.pnOsWindowsNote: Win 10 / 11. Cheapest option: a $150 mini-PC stays on permanently next to the printer.
kitchen.pnOsMacosNote: macOS 11+. Use an old Mac mini or any spare Mac.
kitchen.pnOsLinuxNote: Debian/Ubuntu/RHEL. Headless install on a small server works fine.
kitchen.pnOsRpiNote: Pre-built Raspbian image. ~$60 hardware, lowest-cost dedicated print bridge.
kitchen.pnStep4Body: In the PrintNode dashboard, go to Account → API Keys, click Create API Key, give it any name (e.g. "Fee Free Ordering"), and copy the long string that appears. You only see it once — save it somewhere safe before navigating away.
kitchen.pnStep5Title: Paste the key below, click Test Connection
kitchen.pnStep5Body: Paste the API key into the field below. Click Test Connection. If your account name shows up + your printer appears in the list, you're done. Pick the printer, save settings, fire a test print.
kitchen.pnHeadsUp: Heads up: the PrintNode app must stay running on the device with the printer for receipts to print. If that device goes offline or PrintNode is closed, jobs queue up until it's back online. Most owners leave the PrintNode device powered on 24/7 (it sips electricity).
kitchen.pnApiKeyPlaceholderSaved: API key saved — enter new value to replace
kitchen.pnApiKeySaved: API key is saved. Leave blank to keep it.
kitchen.pnFindKey: Find your API key at <link>app.printnode.com → Account → API Keys</link>
kitchen.pnNoPrinters: No printers found. Make sure PrintNode Desktop is running on the computer with your printer.
kitchen.pnTestPrintSent: Test print sent! Check your printer.
kitchen.pnDiagnosticsHint: Use these to identify the right protocol for your printer. Start with Plain Text — if that works, try ESC/POS or StarPRNT.
kitchen.pnDiagPlainDesc: No control codes — just readable text
kitchen.pnDiagEscposDesc: Bold, center, large text, cut (Epson/generic protocol)
kitchen.pnDiagStarprntDesc: Bold, sizes, inverse, black bars, alignment — each with PASS/FAIL label (Star StarPRNT mode)
kitchen.pnDiagStarBoldDesc: Tests ESC E (StarPRNT), ESC F/H (Star Line), and ESC ! (ESC/POS) side-by-side — print to see which bold command works on your printer
kitchen.pnDiagSent: Diagnostic sent ({n} bytes, job #{id}). Check printer.
kitchen.pnLangEscposDesc: Epson ESC/POS — for Epson, Bixolon, and most generic thermal printers
kitchen.pnLangStarprntDesc: Star Micronics StarPRNT protocol — uses ESC E n for bold. Run 'STAR BOLD TEST' to verify.
kitchen.pnLangStarLine: Star Line Mode — Star TSP143 (Star Line)
kitchen.pnLangStarLineDesc: Star Micronics Star Line mode — uses ESC F/H for bold. Try this if StarPRNT bold is not visible.
kitchen.pnKitchenCopySub: Items, modifiers, and notes for kitchen staff
kitchen.pnCustomerCopySub: Full receipt with prices for the customer
kitchen.pnLargeOrderNumberSub: Double-height order # at top of kitchen copy
kitchen.pnAutoPrintSub: Sends receipt to printer when order is accepted
